content.text: Treasury desk. Half of this game is a balance sheet. These are its dials.

Credits are XP. The economy is the progression system and the pressure system, which makes this page the GM's console: what comes in, what drains out, and the one ratio that tells you whether the machine is running.
Income
Mission pay — the base contract rate, the spine of every career.
Field income — carve yields and salvage. Yields and their clocks live on Carving; salvage substitutes for components at GM-assessed value under The Material Cost Law — loot is liquidity.

Profession income — rank-ramped, always. Income ramps Sideline → Established → Name; it never starts at Established rates. A fresh trade earns like a fresh trade. (Rates and perks live on each profession's page.)

Trade-track wealth is a feature, not a leak. Trade careers are period-gated where combat careers are credit-gated: they end rich and modestly installed, because their growth came only from spare periods. That liquidity becomes the squad's base fund, gear float, and safety margin. Let it.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: No rebuild doc prints a mission pay scale — base pay by contract tier is unpriced. Phase 6 tuned shares of pay, never pay itself, and the legacy Contract Generation tables are pre-rebuild. Rent and lifestyle rates are likewise unprinted.
The Maintenance-Share Table (GM Side)
Career-length figures, final tuning. Players see the maintenance column on The Creditor Table; the full table — mortality and growth included — is yours.
*Trade-track installs are period-gated, not credit-gated — growth from spare periods only.

How to read it: the creditor bills behave as designed. The Furnace and the marksman pay the most and feel it in their growth columns; the clean fighter banks power and the rider banks risk. The Berserker at ride 3 with a real squad sits at 46% career mortality — dangerous, playable, chosen, and visible in advance. Solo riders still die. They should.

content.label: THE HARDNESS GATE
content.text: Treat-vs-train is the same wallet and the same calendar: treatment credits and downtime are never training credits and downtime. The gate holds when maintenance share lands in the 25–55% band across combat archetypes — at that ratio, treat-vs-train stays a real decision at every stage of a career. That band is the dial this page exists to watch.
Per-Drop Arithmetic

Per-drop odds × expected drops × the squad's reach = the career. At Grit +3: two untreated scars is 65% per drop; four is 45%. Ride 2 is professional risk. Ride 3 is gambling. Ride 4+ is the legend track. The roll itself lives on The Staircase; the texture lives on The Actuary Page.
Drains

rows: Principle on Armor (The Material Cost Law); rates unprinted

content.text: Detox: The Provider Ladder
Detox clears Saturation, priced per point by provider tier (Saturation itself — see Charge & Saturation).
A Street Doc with the Detox Protocol perk undercuts the street rate and clears faster — that perk and its numbers live on Street Doc.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The consolidation prices detox "by provider tier," but only the street tier (40 cr/pt) is printed. Clinic and corp rates — and whatever besides price distinguishes them — await a ruling.

WELLSPRING MEMBER SERVICES: Saturation management plans are now available at rates competitive with unlicensed providers. Member biodata is retained for quality, training, and underwriting purposes. Primarily underwriting.
Facilities

Facility floors, payback rates, and the funnel law live on The Base. The flagship investments a career can become — stat 10, tree mastery, a Name, a raised beast — are priced on one scale on The Anchor Table.
